evals.prompt: We need a marketing plan for our B2B SaaS relaunch. Help me choose between landing-page CRO, lifecycle email work, and paid ads, then give me one implementation-ready brief.
evals.expected_output: The skill should stay in general marketing mode, choose an appropriate sub-skill or sequence, and produce a KPI-aware marketing brief rather than generic brainstorming.
assertions: Output identifies a single primary objective or KPI
Output selects or names a relevant marketing sub-skill category
Output includes an implementation-oriented deliverable or checklist

evals.prompt: Our prompt pack still references marketing-skills-collection. Use it to map this request into the right lane: improve onboarding emails for trial users who sign up but never activate.
evals.expected_output: The skill should remain a valid legacy-compatible entry point, route to a lifecycle/email/onboarding lane, and keep the response measurable.
assertions: Output treats the request as a valid fit for marketing-skills-collection
Output routes to onboarding, lifecycle, or email-sequence work
Output mentions a measurement concept such as activation, CTR, or conversion

evals.prompt: Please review our Steam page before Next Fest. Wishlists are weak and I want feedback on our capsule, screenshots, trailer, and demo timing.
evals.expected_output: The skill should recognize this as a niche Steam launch-ops request and defer to the dedicated steam-store-launch-ops workflow instead of pretending the general marketing catalog is the best fit.
assertions: Output indicates that a dedicated Steam-specific skill is a better fit
Output references at least one Steam-native concept such as wishlists, capsule, tags, demo, or Next Fest
Output does not frame the request as only a generic launch-strategy or paid-ads task
